name: strong-match-with-noise
desc: A dominant on-topic memory plus marginal recall noise below the injection threshold. Keep the strong matches, drop the weak tail.

candidates.content: Access tokens expire after 15m; refresh tokens rotate on use and are stored hashed.
Sessions live in Redis keyed by user id with a 30 day TTL.
Use structured slog with request_id on every line.
GitHub Actions caches the go build cache keyed on go.sum.

name: sparse-topic-moderate-scores
desc: A niche topic: two moderately-confident matches just above the threshold plus sub-threshold noise. Should inject both relevant ones and drop the noise.

candidates.content: MSC_UPSTREAM sentinel prevents nested-proxy infinite loops.
A child msc reads the real upstream from the sentinel env var.
The frontend uses Tailwind for styling.

name: duplicate-heavy
desc: The same fact recalled three ways. Near-duplicate removal should keep one and not waste budget on restatements.

candidates.content: the user prefers table driven tests with subtests and t dot run
the user prefers table driven tests with subtests and t dot run always
the user prefers table driven tests with subtests and t dot run for everything
production runs on kubernetes with helm managed releases

name: all-noise
desc: Recall surfaced only marginally-relevant memories, all below the confidence threshold. The right answer is to inject NOTHING this turn.

candidates.content: The office wifi password rotates monthly.
Coffee machine is on the third floor.

name: mixed-relevance-tight-budget
desc: Several relevant memories but a tight budget. Budget packing should spend tokens on the highest-scored relevant memories first.

candidates.content: The exchanges table partitions by day and indexes (vault, created_at). This is the primary read path for recall.
Rows older than 90 days are pruned nightly by a cron job that batches deletes in chunks of 10k.
Daily logical dumps to object storage retained for 30 days; restore tested weekly.
The mobile app uses React Native.

name: clean-high-relevance
desc: Everything recalled is relevant and distinct. Selection should keep all of them; precision and recall both 1.

candidates.content: msc intercepts in ServeHTTP and buffers the body when the path matches capture paths.
SSE responses tee through streamCapture while accumulating text deltas.
Exchanges enqueue to a buffered channel and a worker batches up to 10 every 2s.

name: cold-open-no-signal
desc: A generic opening question with no relevant stored memory; recall returns only low-cosine chatter. Injecting anything is pure noise — the gate should suppress.

candidates.content: The user sometimes says hi before asking.
It rained last Tuesday.

name: single-strong-hit
desc: Exactly one clearly relevant memory well above the threshold. Should inject just that one.

candidates.content: The API enforces 100 req/min per token with a sliding window.
Brand primary is #1a73e8.
